_description: Indian Law Enforcement Rules — maps document types to mandatory Indian statutory requirements. The IRE engine checks these rules during generation and validation.

enforcement_rules.law: Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952 – S.6
enforcement_rules.message: Employment contracts must address EPF contributions. Criminal liability for employer under EPF Act S.14 if EPF is not deducted.
Salary must not be below applicable minimum wages. Employer faces criminal prosecution for below-minimum wages.

enforcement_rules.law: Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act, 1946; Constitution Art.21
enforcement_rules.message: Employment contracts must include a disciplinary procedure providing opportunity to be heard before adverse action. Violation is grounds for reinstatement.
Employment contracts must address maternity benefits. Denial of maternity leave is a criminal offence under MBA S.21.
Service agreements must address TDS deduction. Payment clause must specify whether S.194C (contracts, 2%) or S.194J (professional, 10%) applies.

enforcement_rules.law: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 – S.31
enforcement_rules.message: Payment clause must require GST-compliant tax invoices with GSTIN, SAC/HSN code, and GST amount. Non-compliant invoices block input tax credit.
Loan agreements must address TDS on interest under S.194A (10% TDS when interest > Rs.40,000 p.a. for resident individuals).
Interest rate in loan agreement must not be usurious. Courts may reduce interest rates above commercially reasonable levels.
Leases for a term exceeding 12 months must be compulsorily registered. Unregistered leases are inadmissible as evidence under Registration Act S.49.

enforcement_rules.law: Indian Stamp Act, 1899 – S.3; State Stamp Acts
enforcement_rules.message: Rental and lease agreements must be executed on adequately stamped paper. Unstamped instruments are inadmissible under Indian Stamp Act S.35.
Where annual rent exceeds Rs.2,40,000, the tenant must deduct TDS at 10% on commercial rent (S.194I). Rent payment clause should address TDS obligations.
Shareholders Agreement cannot override Companies Act provisions, MoA or AoA. Any SHA provision inconsistent with Companies Act is void.

Copyright assignment must be in writing and signed by the assignor (Copyright Act S.19). Service agreements must include written IP assignment — unlike employment, contractor IP does NOT auto-vest in client.

enforcement_rules.law: Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 – S.20; BALCO v Kaiser Aluminium (SC)
enforcement_rules.message: Arbitration clause must specify the seat of arbitration. Without seat, Part I vs Part II applicability is ambiguous and Indian courts may lack supervisory jurisdiction.

enforcement_rules.law: SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015 – Regulation 3
enforcement_rules.message: NDAs involving listed companies or their advisors must explicitly address UPSI obligations under SEBI PIT Regulations 2015.

enforcement_rules.law: Indian Stamp Act, 1899 – S.3; State Stamp Acts
enforcement_rules.message: All contracts must be executed on adequately stamped paper. Stamp duty varies by document type, value and state. Unstamped instruments are inadmissible (S.35).
